narrative.headline: 2022 Toyota Corolla: electrical issues lead the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 93 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is the most reported area at 20%, followed by the power train (15%), and the service brakes and engine (14% each). No fires or deaths are reported. The brakes and air bags draw fewer complaints but carry the most serious outcomes, so check both closely on a test drive.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 19 reports (20%), with 1 injury and no crashes or fires. The leading complaint by volume.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 14 reports (15%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Watch for loss of acceleration while driving.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 13 reports (14%), but among the most serious by outcome: 3 crashes and 3 injuries. Test the brakes thoroughly.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 13 reports (14%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Watch for the engine not responding to the accelerator or shutting off unexpectedly.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: Only 9 reports (10%), but the most crash-linked of any area: 8 crashes and 5 injuries. Worth a careful look given the severity.
